alternative_paths_each_separate_authoriz.description: Keep the RUN_BOOK on file; no operator fetch initiated. Trading-bot research track pauses on s14-d1.

alternative_paths_each_separate_authoriz.authorization_phrase: Authorize s14-d1 cash-equity 3-name basket RSI(3) bi-directional Tier-N spec PLAN only — bound by DR10 v2.

Requires fresh Tier-N spec PLAN turn (operator-side OHLCV fetch for selected names also required at later phase).

authorization_phrase_typed_by_operator: Authorize s14-d1 multi-instrument availability probe + DR9 audit RESULT SEALING only.
decline_reason: All 4 prerequisite operator-captured artifacts specified in RUN_BOOK §4.2 are MISSING from disk. The directory data/s14_d1_mnq_mes_mym_m2k_databento_long_history/raw/ does not exist. Result-sealing binds captured CSV sha256 into a permanent sealed result; without captured CSVs the only options are fabrication (forbidden), placeholder-sealing (fake seal), or controller-side fetch (forbidden by no_databento_call + no_databento_api_key_access + no_network_io). All three forbidden options would violate framework integrity. Fail-closed is the correct outcome.
report_kind: Authorization decline memo (fail-closed). NOT a SEAL. Records that the operator-typed 'Authorize s14-d1 multi-instrument availability probe + DR9 audit RESULT SEALING only.' was DECLINED by the controller because none of the prerequisite operator-captured artifacts specified in the RUN_BOOK §4.2 exist on disk.
title: s14-d1 multi-instrument availability probe + DR9 audit RESULT SEALING authorization DECLINED — prerequisite operator-captured artifacts missing (fail-closed)
unblock_path: Step 1 (operator action; outside controller scope): Operator executes the Databento fetch per RUN_BOOK §4 (executor = operator; DATABENTO_API_KEY in operator's environment only; controller never reads). The 4 expected files must land at the expected paths under data/s14_d1_mnq_mes_mym_m2k_databento_long_history/raw/.
Step 2 (operator confirmation): Operator verifies each CSV exists at the expected path and the manifest file exists with sha256 entries for each CSV (per RUN_BOOK §4.5).

Step 3 (operator-issued authorization): Operator re-issues the authorization phrase `Authorize s14-d1 multi-instrument availability probe + DR9 audit RESULT SEALING only.` Controller can then verify captured artifact shas + perform the pre-authored DR9 audit per RUN_BOOK §3 + seal the result.
If at any point the operator aborts the fetch (e.g., Databento availability error, API rate limit, network issue): do NOT modify the RUN_BOOK; re-attempt the fetch with a clean environment.
what_this_memo_does_NOT_do: Does NOT seal any audit result.
Does NOT perform any DR9 audit (no source data to audit).
Does NOT make a Databento call.
Does NOT promote s14-d1 to DRAFT or any subsequent phase.
Does NOT modify any existing sealed artifact (s11-d1 / s12-d1 / s13-d1 / framework_dr10_revision_seal_v2 all byte-stable; s14-d1 PLAN at 5376de7 byte-stable; s14-d1 RUN_BOOK at 13ff641 byte-stable).
Does NOT revive s13-d1 / s12-d1 / any parked candidate.
Does NOT advance the s14-d1 lifecycle state (state remains S14_D1_AVAILABILITY_PROBE_DR9_AUDIT_PENDING_OPERATOR_FETCH).
why_this_decline_is_correct: Result-sealing binds the captured CSV sha256 into a permanent sealed audit result. Without captured CSVs, a sealed result would have no real binding (it would either bind to non-existent files or bind to fabricated values).

Per s14-d1 PLAN hard boundaries and framework-wide invariants: no_databento_call + no_databento_api_key_access + no_network_io are immutable for the controller. The controller cannot perform the fetch unilaterally; that is operator-side action only.

Per framework invariants no_profitability_claim + no fabrication: the controller cannot synthesize DR9 audit results without source CSVs. Pretending all 3 symbols pass DR9 without auditing actual data would be a fabricated seal.
Sealing an empty placeholder would create a fake seal with no binding evidentiary content — would violate framework integrity and create a precedent for empty seals.
The fail-closed decline is the intentional gate behavior documented in the RUN_BOOK §4.5 (post-fetch action requires operator to confirm all 4 files exist before requesting result-sealing).

This pattern is byte-equivalent to the prior B006_002 result-sealing decline (recorded in this session's brain memory at decisions.md entry for 2026-05-26 'B006_002 result-sealing authorization DECLINED - prerequisite missing'). The framework discipline is consistent.
